label: Netlify
description: Connect to Netlify to manage sites, trigger deployments, and inspect build logs.

form.title: Connect Netlify
form.subtitle: Paste your Netlify personal access token to let Anton manage your sites and deployments.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Generate a long-lived token in Netlify User Settings → Applications → Personal access tokens and paste it here. No OAuth setup required — the simplest path.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Netlify Personal Access Token

1. Log in to [Netlify](https://app.netlify.com) and click your avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Go to **User Settings** (not a team's settings — your personal account).
3. In the left sidebar, click **Applications**.
4. Scroll to the **Personal access tokens** section and click **New access token**.
5. Give the token a description like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
6. Click **Generate token**. Copy the token — it is shown **only once**.
7. Paste the token into the **Personal access token** field below.

## Notes

- The token inherits your account's permissions across all teams and sites you belong to.
- If you lose the token, generate a new one and update it in Anton — old tokens cannot be retrieved.
- To revoke access, return to **User Settings → Applications → Personal access tokens** and click **Delete** next to the token.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Personal access token
fields.description: Found in Netlify → User Settings → Applications → Personal access tokens. Shown only once on creation.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use your own Netlify OAuth application to authorize Anton via browser-based OAuth. Requires creating an OAuth app in Netlify team settings first.
methods.how_to:
## Set up a Netlify OAuth Application

1. Log in to [Netlify](https://app.netlify.com) and open your **Team** dashboard.
2. Go to **Team Settings → OAuth → OAuth Applications → New OAuth app**.
3. Set the application name to `Anton` and the redirect URI to `http://127.0.0.1` (or whatever Anton's local callback is).
4. Click **Save**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown on the app detail page.
5. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. Anton will open a browser tab to Netlify's authorization page — approve access.

## Notes

- OAuth tokens are shorter-lived than personal access tokens; Anton will refresh them automatically.
- Netlify OAuth apps are scoped at the team level — ensure the app belongs to the right team.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth client ID
fields.description: From Netlify Team Settings → OAuth → OAuth Applications. Copy the Client ID of your app.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID. Shown once when the OAuth app is created in Netlify.
